steps.title: Understand what the data shows and what must follow
steps.content: The survey found that package aesthetics = low importance and speedy delivery + tracking = high importance to customer experience. Prior studies link customer experience to customer retention (repeat purchases). The blank must apply those importance ratings to a conclusion about retention.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice and pick the match
steps.content:
• Choice A — there is a need to conduct additional surveys with the same group of participants to further understand why package aesthetics do not influence their overall satisfaction with online retailers.: Calls for new research not implied by the findings; the study already drew its conclusion. ✗
  • Choice B — the likelihood of customers making repeat purchases from a specific online retailer is more closely related to the efficiency and transparency of the retailer's delivery process than to the retailer's package design.: Directly maps high-importance factors (delivery speed, tracking) to retention (repeat purchases) and low-importance factors (aesthetics/package design) as less influential — exactly what the data and prior-studies link support. ✓
  • Choice C — when leaving online reviews of retailers, customers are most concerned with retailers' ability to deliver goods quickly and to provide easy ways of tracking those goods during shipment.: Shifts focus to online reviews, which are never mentioned in the passage. ✗
  • Choice D — although online retailers are unlikely to increase customer retention by improving their package designs, improved designs may attract new customers.: The “attract new customers” claim is unsupported; the study only addressed existing customer experience and retention. ✗
That is the correct answer.
